Did You Know? Windows' Shutdown Isn't Actually Shutting Down
Newer Windows versions ship with 'Fast Startup' on by default — Shutdown actually dumps system state to a file and reloads it next boot. Why, and how to turn it off.
Tired of Windows 10 Forcing Updates on You? Here's How to Disable Auto-Updates

Windows 10's forced update behavior is the worst — slowing your machine mid-work, throwing random new bugs, or worst of all bluescreen on a botched update. This post walks through disabling the Windows Update service from the Services UI. Heads up — this also disables security updates, so only do it if you understand the security trade-off.
Notes on Switching From Windows 10 to macOS

Recently moved my dev environment to macOS. This post is a few notes on what surprised me in the Windows-to-macOS transition — modifier key remapping, input method switching, full-width vs. half-width characters, trackpad gesture differences, and the reversed scroll direction.
